persistence.portable: database-free shareable review link stores the review packet in the URL hash
persistence.shared: shared persistence requires Vercel Functions plus KV, Supabase, Neon, or another database
persistence.reason: A static Vercel page can preserve edits in one browser and export/import packets, but it cannot synchronize teammate comments across browsers without a server-backed store.

vercel.teamHandoffTemplate: Please review your Vexy section before rehearsal. Open your link, click Review / Edit, edit your private speaker notes, add pinned comments where something should change, then click Copy review link and send it back.
vercel.handoff: Open the deployed URL with ?edit=1&owner=<speaker>, click Review / Edit, pin comments, edit copy, then copy a shareable review link or export the review packet.

speakers.name: Grace
speakers.role: Premise, physical VEX body, and why the self-model is language-grounded
noteSuggestions: Open by naming the premise: Self-Modeling Machines, in Language.
Point to VEX V5, Raspberry Pi, AprilTag, and the yellow ball as the physical grounding.
Close by saying grab, pull, and throw make the learning loop visible.
speakers.suggestion: Make the opening explicitly about Self-Modeling Machines, in Language, then show how VEX V5, Raspberry Pi, AprilTags, and the yellow ball make the premise physical.

speakers.name: David Taylor
speakers.role: Small learning loop, Generational Loop, research synthesis, and honest future path
noteSuggestions: Say the small loop is plan, execute, analyze telemetry, then start over.
Explain the Generational Loop as the future body-redesign layer.
Frame the novelty as combining self-modeling, LLM morphology, and hardware evidence.
speakers.suggestion: Keep David out of the hardware intro. David explains why the learning loop is evidence-driven and how it connects prior self-modeling, LLM morphology, and hardware self-improvement research.

speakers.name: Erick
speakers.role: Trusted motion, bounded commands, ack/fault states, and safety evidence
noteSuggestions: Use the phrase trusted motion: bounded commands plus evidence.
Name the real-world messiness: slip, drift, camera errors, and moving targets.
Explain why strict command vocabulary lets the LLM reason without freelancing against motors.
speakers.suggestion: Make Erick's copy about trusted motion: the LLM reasons, but robot motion goes through bounded contracts, watchdogs, clamps, and proof.

speakers.name: Jake
speakers.role: Perception-to-proof, AprilTag localization, scene coordinates, JSONL evidence, and measurement
noteSuggestions: Define the perception path: camera frames, AprilTags, yellow-ball detections, scene coordinates, telemetry, JSONL.
Say raw vision is not enough; localized scene evidence makes the gap meaningful.
Connect AprilTags, yellow-ball confidence, and coordinate formatting to grounded observation.
speakers.suggestion: Make Jake's copy explain why a run only matters when raw perception becomes localized, structured evidence: AprilTags, object positions, scene state, telemetry, JSONL, gap, and update.
